title: nepeta mallophora subsp. microglandulosa
intro: Nepeta mallophora subsp. microglandulosa is a subspecies of the Nepeta genus, commonly known as catmint or beebread, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. Traditionally, Nepeta mallophora subsp. microglandulosa has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and inflammation due to its antispasmodic, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Nepeta mallophora subsp. microglandulosa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, relieve respiratory congestion, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Nepeta mallophora subsp. microglandulosa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and dried flower arrangements, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
